title: Bengali Aloo Bhaja (Crispy Fried Potatoes)

subs.name: raw banana sliced thin
subs.notes: fry 1 minute longer per side as raw banana is denser

subs.name: cumin seeds (1/4 tsp)
subs.notes: cumin gives a North Indian character; kalonji is the authentic Bengali choice

subs.name: coconut oil for a milder version
subs.notes: heat coconut oil until lightly smoking before adding the potatoes

steps.text: Slice the potatoes into very thin rounds, about 2 to 3 mm thick. A sharp knife or mandoline is ideal. Rinse the slices in cold water to remove excess starch, then pat completely dry with a kitchen cloth.
Heat mustard oil in a tawa or wide frying pan over high flame until it turns pale and a faint smoke appears. Add the kalonji and let it sizzle for 5 seconds.
Working in batches, lay the potato slices in a single layer. Sprinkle turmeric, chilli powder and salt over the top. Fry on high without disturbing for 3 minutes until the undersides are golden.

Flip each slice and fry the other side for 2 to 3 minutes until crisp and golden-brown at the edges. The centres may remain slightly softer than the edges — that contrast is the joy of aloo bhaja. Remove and drain briefly.
Repeat with remaining slices. Serve immediately alongside dal and rice — aloo bhaja loses its crispness within 10 minutes and does not reheat well.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep water away from hot oil, lower food gently, avoid overfilling the vessel and never leave frying unattended.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: mustard-oil → coconut oil for a milder version (heat coconut oil until lightly smoking before adding the potatoes); kalonji → cumin seeds (1/4 tsp) (cumin gives a North Indian character; kalonji is the authentic Bengali choice); potato → raw banana sliced thin (fry 1 minute longer per side as raw banana is denser)
